name: Pic de la Dona Loop Trail
name_original: Pic de la Dona – trail depuis Le Mantet – Parc naturel régional des Pyrénées Catalanes
description: This difficult 11.6-mile jogging trail to Pic de la Dona from Le Mantet offers vast Pyrenean views and a challenging 4238-foot climb.
region_intro: Jogging routes around Mantet are situated in the Pyrenean mountains at an altitude of 1540 meters, offering varied terrain for runners. The region is characterized by rugged peaks, green forests, and rolling meadows, providing diverse elevations. Trails wind through natural landscapes, along streams, and across mountain reliefs, including sections within the National Nature Reserve of Mantet. This environment ensures a pristine setting for running, with options ranging from paths through meadows to more challenging mountain ascents. Best jogging routes around Mantet The most popular jogging route is Bastiments y Pic de la Dona - trail run circular through the Natural Park of the Headwaters of Ter and Freser, a 6.4 miles (10.3 km) trail that takes 2 hours 7 minutes to complete. This difficult circular route traverses a natural park, offering a challenging trail running experience. Another top favourite among local runners is Pic de la Dona Loop Trail, a difficult 11.6 miles (18.6 km) path. This loop trail features significant elevation changes, providing a strenuous workout with expansive mountain views. Local runners also love the Pic de la Dona (2,702 m) – Collado de la Marrana loop from Vallter 2000, a 9.1 miles (14.6 km) trail leading through high mountain passes and towards a prominent peak, often completed in about 3 hours 9 minutes. Jogging around Mantet is defined by Pyrenean mountain scenery, diverse trails, and the preserved environment of the National Nature Reserve. The network offers options for experienced trail runners seeking significant elevation gain. The routes in Mantet are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from more than 5 reviews. More than 2000 runners have used komoot to explore Mantet's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Mantet?
A: Mantet, nestled in the Pyrenean mountains, offers a wide selection of over 80 running routes. These trails cater to various preferences, from paths through green forests and rolling meadows to challenging mountain ascents.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the jogging trails in Mantet?
A: The terrain around Mantet is incredibly diverse, characterized by rugged peaks, green forests, and rolling meadows. You'll find varied elevations, with trails winding along streams and across mountain reliefs. Many routes are within the National Nature Reserve of Mantet, ensuring a pristine natural setting.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly running routes in Mantet?
A: While Mantet is known for its challenging mountain terrain, with most routes rated as difficult, there are a few moderate options available. These can be a good starting point for runners looking for less strenuous experiences while still enjoying the Pyrenean scenery. However, most routes involve significant elevation changes.

Q: What are some notable natural features or landmarks I might see while running in Mantet?
A: Mantet's routes offer breathtaking views of the Pyrenean mountain scenery. You might encounter features like the nearby Carança Gorges, known for their dramatic corniche and footbridges. Specific highlights include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/260391" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Carança Corniche&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/873604" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Footbridges of the Carançà Gorge&lt;/a&gt;. The region also boasts high mountain passes and the untouched beauty of the National Nature Reserve.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in the Mantet area?
A: Yes, Mantet offers several circular running routes that allow you to start and finish in the same location. An example is the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e957096809/bastiments-y-pic-de-la-dona-trail-run-circular-through-the-natural-park-of-the-headwaters-of-ter-and-freser" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bastiments y Pic de la Dona - trail run circular through the Natural Park of the Headwaters of Ter and Freser&lt;/a&gt;, which is 10.3 km long and traverses a natural park.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about the trails in Mantet?
A: The running routes in Mantet are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 8 reviews. Runners frequently praise the challenging nature of the trails, the significant elevation gains, and the expansive, breathtaking mountain views that make for a rewarding experience.

Q: Is Mantet suitable for trail running with dogs?
A: Mantet is located within the National Nature Reserve, which often has specific regulations regarding pets to protect wildlife. While many outdoor areas in the Pyrenees are dog-friendly, it's advisable to check local signage or contact the reserve management for specific rules on trails within the protected area to ensure a responsible and enjoyable run with your dog.

Q: What is the best time of year for jogging in Mantet?
A: The best time for jogging in Mantet generally aligns with the warmer months, from late spring to early autumn, when the mountain trails are clear of snow and ice. The village itself is at 1540m altitude, and many routes climb higher, so conditions can vary. Always check local weather and trail conditions before heading out, especially for higher-altitude routes.

Q: Are there any running routes that offer particularly good viewpoints?
A: Absolutely. Mantet's location in the Pyrenees means many routes offer superb panoramic views. Trails that climb to mountain passes or prominent peaks, such as those towards Pic de la Dona or Col del Pal, are renowned for their rewarding vistas of the surrounding rugged peaks and valleys.

Q: How challenging are the running routes in Mantet?
A: The running routes in Mantet are generally considered challenging, with a significant number of difficult trails. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e974178577/pic-de-la-dona-loop-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pic de la Dona Loop Trail&lt;/a&gt; is an 18.6 km difficult path featuring substantial elevation changes, providing a strenuous workout with expansive mountain views. Runners should be prepared for considerable ascents and descents.

Q: Are there any facilities like cafes or accommodation directly in Mantet?
A: Mantet is a small village situated at 1540 meters altitude. While specific establishments are not detailed in the route information, its status as a village implies basic amenities might be available. For specific cafes, pubs, or accommodation, it's recommended to check local listings for Mantet prior to your visit.

Q: What is the average duration for a running route in Mantet?
A: The duration of running routes in Mantet varies significantly based on distance and difficulty. For instance, the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e957096809/bastiments-y-pic-de-la-dona-trail-run-circular-through-the-natural-park-of-the-headwaters-of-ter-and-freser" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bastiments y Pic de la Dona - trail run circular through the Natural Park of the Headwaters of Ter and Freser&lt;/a&gt; typically takes around 2 hours and 7 minutes to complete, while longer routes can take 3 hours or more.

The Pic de la Dona is a mountain of 2,702 meters that is located between the municipality of Setcases, in the region of Ripollès, and the municipality of Mentet, in the Conflent, the second in northern Catalonia

faqs.question: Is the Pic de la Dona trail from Le Mantet suitable for jogging or beginners?
faqs.answer: Despite being categorized for 'jogging' in some systems, this specific route from Le Mantet to Pic de la Dona is a demanding undertaking, better suited for experienced hikers and mountaineers. It involves significant elevation gain (around 1300 meters), steep, rocky sections, and can include boulder fields. It requires very good fitness, sure-footedness, and sturdy shoes. It is not recommended for casual jogging or beginners.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the way to Pic de la Dona from Le Mantet?
faqs.answer: The terrain is challenging and varied. You'll encounter steep ascents, rocky paths, and potentially boulder fields, especially as you gain altitude. Some sections may not be clearly marked, requiring good orientation skills. The path following the GR10 from Mantet towards the peak is known to be rugged.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trailhead in Le Mantet?
faqs.answer: Le Mantet is an isolated village that serves as a starting point for several mountain walks. You should be able to find parking within or near the village to begin your ascent towards Pic de la Dona. Specific parking areas are usually well-indicated for hikers.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route to Pic de la Dona?
faqs.answer: The trail offers extremely vast and magnificent panoramic views of the Catalan Pyrenees. You'll pass through diverse landscapes, including forests and high-altitude meadows. Notable points include a Stone Refuge Near Pas de les Soques. The summit, Pic de la Dona (2,702 m), provides impressive vistas of dramatic mountain ranges and glaciated valleys.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The best time to hike this trail is typically during the summer months when conditions are most favorable. However, even in summer, the trail is strenuous. In winter, the ascent can involve technical alpinism, requiring specialized equipment like helmets, crampons, and ice axes due to snow couloirs with steep passages.

faqs.question: Is the Pic de la Dona trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: The trail is located within the Parc naturel régional des Pyrénées Catalanes and specifically within the Réserve Naturelle de Mantet. While dogs are generally allowed in many parts of the regional park, specific regulations may apply within the nature reserve. It is crucial to keep dogs on a leash to protect local wildlife, especially chamois and isards, and to check for any specific restrictions for the Réserve Naturelle de Mantet before your visit.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or is there an entrance fee for the Réserve Naturelle de Mantet?
faqs.answer: The trail is situated within the Réserve Naturelle de Mantet, which is part of the larger Parc naturel régional des Pyrénées Catalanes. Generally, there are no entrance fees for accessing these natural areas. However, as a protected reserve, specific regulations apply regarding activities, waste, and wildlife protection. No specific hiking permits are usually required for day hikes, but always respect the natural environment and any posted rules.

faqs.question: What wildlife might I spot on the trail?
faqs.answer: The area is known for its exceptional biodiversity. Hikers have the potential to spot various flora and fauna, including chamois and isards, particularly in the higher alpine meadows and rocky areas. Keep an eye out for diverse bird species as well.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route to Pic de la Dona from Le Mantet intersects with several other trails. Notably, it follows sections of the GR10 and also passes through parts of 'De la Manche aux Pyrénées 24', 'Sournia - Bourg-Madame', and 'La traversée des Pyrénées - sec.2'.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities or accommodation options in Le Mantet?
faqs.answer: Le Mantet is an isolated and charming village. While it serves as a starting point for challenging mountain walks, facilities are limited. It's advisable to carry all necessary supplies. For accommodation, you might find gîtes or small inns, but it's best to book in advance, especially during peak season. There are no cafes or pubs directly on the trail itself.

faqs.question: Is this a loop trail or an out-and-back route?
faqs.answer: The description of the route from Le Mantet to Pic de la Dona typically implies an out-and-back journey to the summit. While there might be variations or connections to other trails, the direct ascent to Pic de la Dona from Mantet is generally treated as a return trip along the same path.
